title: typo
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
/approve
LGTM
Thanks @pwright 
You need to sign  your commits.

git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force
@rhatdan  I've signed commit, but workflows failing, next step?

@pwright: Cannot trigger testing until a trusted user reviews the PR and leaves an `/ok-to-test` message.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23649#issuecomment-2301743744):

>/retest


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes-sigs/prow](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/prow/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
rebased in the hope of landing this PR?
@cevich @lsm5 @edsantiago @baude Anyone have an idea why this is not merging?

@kwilczynski: changing LGTM is restricted to collaborators

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23649#issuecomment-2345768562):

>/approve
>/lgtm


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes-sigs/prow](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/prow/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *kwilczynski*, *pwright*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @cevich @lsm5 @edsantiago @baude Anyone have an idea why this is not merging?

@rhatdan, try closing and re-opening the issue. Sometimes helps to poke the bot that got stuck...

seems to be in merge pool now. I hope the `Total Success` task doesn't block it. I also don't see any cirrus tests. Cirrus tests probably aren't required unless that error message is used somewhere in tests.

This is unexpected. The change is in a `.go` file, which should trigger Cirrus. I didn't get to see what was happening before the close/reopen trick.

@pwright I'm really sorry for the inconvenience. Could I ask you to rebase and repush one more time, so I can watch what happens? And if you repush, could you also fix the rest of the line ("osascript" -> "as a script")  just for completeness's sake? Kind thanks.
